title: Pre CI if not aliens then what were Adam and Lilith
display_title: Pre CI if not aliens then what were Adam and Lilith
wikitext:
[]: Pre-CI, if not aliens, then what were Adam and Lilith?             Quote Post by Lavinius » Tue Jan 13, 2015 11:13 pm To my memory, certain people have disliked the idea that Adam and Lilith were space aliens. My question is, then what were those people expecting them to be before the CI was translated?

[]:             Quote Post by Sicarius VI » Tue Jan 13, 2015 11:19 pm I think that it is explained in one of the spin-off games that Adam and Lilth where created from the First Ancestral Race. Like Adam and Lilth are halves of the Race and if they were to combine they would recreate the First Ancestral Race(which is what GNR becomes). In a sense they are space aliens, but more detailed they are two halves of a god like alien race.

[]:             Quote Post by Lavinius » Tue Jan 13, 2015 11:31 pm Sicarius VI wrote: I think that it is explained in one of the spin-off games that Adam and Lilth where created from the First Ancestral Race. Like Adam and Lilth are halves of the Race and if they were to combine they would recreate the First Ancestral Race(which is what GNR becomes). In a sense they are space aliens, but more detailed they are two halves of a god like alien race.

[]:             Quote Post by Sicarius VI » Tue Jan 13, 2015 11:31 pm What does "CI" mean?

[]:             Quote Post by Monk Ed » Tue Jan 13, 2015 11:34 pm Classified Information. http://wiki.evageeks.org/Classified_Information_%28Translation%29

[]:             Quote Post by Sicarius VI » Tue Jan 13, 2015 11:44 pm So in other words pre-2006 before the CI came out what did people think Adam and Lilth were? *Thinking....Thinking* *Raises finger, but has second thought and puts it down* Beings that where created with the Earth? Man I realize how much the Classified Information cleared up.

[]:             Quote Post by Monk Ed » Tue Jan 13, 2015 11:49 pm Even before the CI lots of people assumed they were aliens. As for me, I don't think I thought particularly deeply about the matter, but one possibility I considered was that they were simply ancient, possibly supernatural, possibly even Biblical beings as natural to the Earth (if not more so) as the rest of us.

[]:             Quote Post by Kamon-san » Wed Jan 14, 2015 12:01 am Monk Ed wrote: As for me, I don't think I thought particularly deeply about the matter, but one possibility I considered was that they were simply ancient, possibly supernatural, possibly even Biblical beings as natural to the Earth (if not more so) as the rest of us.

[]:             Quote Post by Mr. Tines » Wed Jan 14, 2015 2:25 am Knowing that Gainax were a bunch of science fiction fans who got their start in convention promo material, and reading the show with that context, episode 21 pretty much states outright that they were of some Forerunner race. It's the sort of thing that I'd expect any moderately avid reader of SF to have picked up on, Forerunner races of various types being such a common furnishing. The CI material in general primarily served as confirmation of what could be inferred in that way (the mechanical details of Kyoko's fate being the only truly new information).
